data.title: Trump to unveil plan for tapping 401(k)s for home down payment at Davos next week
data.description:
President Donald Trump will next week introduce a plan that would let 401(k) savers use some of the funds in their retirement plans for down payments on homes, National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett said Friday in a broadcast interview.

"We’re going to allow people to take money out of their 401(k)s and use that for a down payment," he said on Fox Business's "Mornings with Maria" program. "We’re going to allow people to take money out of their 401(k)s and use that for a down payment."

President Trump will deliver a speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, on Wednesday. The president had earlier said that he would announce at the event some initiatives to make housing more affordable for Americans.

Hassett said the mechanics for the plan are still being worked on. "Suppose that you put 10% down on a home, and then you take 10% of the equity of the home and put it in as an asset in your 401(k). Then your 401(k) will grow over time as the value of your house grows," he said.

Trump recently ramped up his initiatives to help make housing more affordable. Last week, he announced that he would ban large institutional investors from buying single-family houses. He also ordered Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) to buy $200B in mortgage bonds, which is expected to lower mortgage rates.

Earlier this month, Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Bill Pulte said the agency provided a list of 20 to 30 proposals aimed at making homes more affordable to the president, and that Trump will decide on which ones to pursue. Other ideas that were proposed last year include 50-year mortgages and portable mortgages.

On Thursday, Freddie Mac said the average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage rate was 6.06% as of Jan. 15, its lowest rate in about three years.

data.title: Trump says he doesn't want to lose Hassett in his current role
data.description:
President Donald Trump, in praising National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett, said on Friday, "I actually want to keep you where you are, if you want to know the truth." 

Hassett is considered one of the frontrunners to be named the next Federal Reserve chair. Jerome Powell's term heading the central bank expires in May, and Trump has said he'll name his pick for Powell's successor in a few weeks.

The president said Hassett was “fantastic” on television on Friday. "If I move him, these Fed guys, certainly the ones we have now, they don't talk much. I would lose you. It's a serious concern to me," he said at the beginning of an event at the White House announcing the administration's plan for rural healthcare.

"We don't want to lose him, Susie," he said in a comment to his chief of staff, Susie Wiles. "We'll see how it all works out," he added.

In prediction markets, former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh is now favored to become the next Fed chair, with a 56% probability on Polymarket. Current Fed Governor Christopher Waller follows with 16% probability, then Hassett at 13%.

data.title: Diana Shipping launches proxy fight at Genco, nominates six directors
data.description:
Diana Shipping (DSX) said Friday it will launch a proxy fight against rival Genco Shipping (GNK), nominating six industry professionals for election to Genco's board at the upcoming shareholders meeting.

The move comes just days after Genco's (GNK) board rejected Diana's (DSX) $20.60/share takeover offer for the 85.2% of the company it does not already own, calling the offer inadequate.

Diana (DSX) said the current Genco (GNK) board has failed to engage constructively and meaningfully evaluate its aquisition proposal, and believes meaningful change is needed to ensure the board seriously evaluates its offer and other opportunities to maximize shareholder value.

Diana (DSX) said it was disappointed that Genco's (GNK) board took six weeks to respond to its offer and still believes in the merits of consolidation in the dry bulk carriers sector, and that shareholders would benefit if the two companies combined their platforms.

data.title: PureCycle and MP Materials top materials stocks in short interest; Greif and ICL show minimal exposure
data.description:
PureCycle Technologies (PCT) and MP Materials (MP) recorded the highest short interest among materials stocks as of Dec 31, 2025.

The materials sector comprises companies involved in chemicals, fertilizers, mining, and paper products. PureCycle Technologies (PCT) led the materials sector, with short interest at 23.80% of its shares outstanding, followed by MP Materials (MP) at 13.62%, reflecting elevated bearish positioning in both names.

PCT is rated Hold by the Seeking Alpha Quant model due to excessive valuation and poor profitability, coupled with weak momentum. MP Materials (MP), also rated Hold, has seen its shares advance 222.8% over the past year. Seeking Alpha Quant suggests MP's valuation is significantly stretched with its Price/Sales ratio at 44, far exceeding the sector median of 2.

At the other end of the spectrum, Greif’s Class B shares (GEF.B) and ICL Group (ICL) were among the least shorted materials stocks, with short interest of just 0.32% and 0.15%, respectively, pointing to relatively stronger investor confidence.

Here are the top 5 most shorted materials stocks, all with a market capitalization above $2 billion (as a % of shares outstanding)

  • PureCycle Technologies, Inc. (PCT): 23.80% short interest
  • MP Materials Corp. (MP): 13.62%
  • Alpha Metallurgical Resources, Inc. (AMR): 11.59%
  • Cleveland-Cliffs Inc. (CLF): 11.43%
  • Quaker Chemical Corporation (KWR): 11.27%

And here are the least shorted materials stocks, with a market capitalization above $2 billion:

  • Ardagh Metal Packaging (AMBP): 0.71% short interest
  • Aura Minerals Inc. (AUGO): 0.51%
  • Ternium S.A. (TX): 0.38%
  • Greif (GEF.B): 0.32%
  • ICL Group (ICL): 0.15%

More on materials stocks, etc.

data.title: Atlassian, Datadog, Fastly in focus as Citi cuts price targets
data.description:
Atlassian (TEAM), Datadog (DDOG) and Fastly (FSLY) were in focus on Friday as Citi cut its price targets on the aforementioned enterprise software companies.

Atlassian's price target was cut to $210 from $240, while Datadog and Fastly's price targets were lowered to $175 and $10, from $200 and $12, respectively. 

Atlassian shares fell 33% in 2025, and are down nearly 18% since the start of the 2026, amid “stubborn” concerns about financial model complexity, AI disruption, executive turnover, “sizable” M&A and seat-pricing model fears, analyst Fatima Boolani said.

“Recently initiated Data Center EOL/EOS timeline has further disrupted financial model mechanics, coming not too long after the Server Support EOL (F4Q24), with an increased upfront rev-rec for Data Center (50% vs. 20% prior) boosting FY26E revenue outlook but in turn creating a tough YoY comp for FY27E,” Boolani wrote. “But more importantly, this change stokes yet more pessimism on underlying cadence and quality of topline growth (outside of pricing increases, model transition/migration, accounting impacts), especially as dev/tech headcount trends have remained muted, with perceived disruption by code assistant platforms having exacerbated these existentially bearish concerns.”

Datadog has also seen its shares underperform in recent memory, as fears about category commoditization, pricing pressures, and OpenAI's (OPENAI) concentrated power weigh. However, Boolani, who has a Buy rating on Datadog, said investor sentiment is mixed.

“From our investor discussions, we’ve also sensed mixed views on the aggregate AI-native exposure, which while clearly attractive and corroborating DDOG’s status as an 'AI Winner' is also being perceived more measuredly, given business cycle volatility and outsized churn risk considerations in this crop of customers – almost akin to the ZIRP-era startups to whom DDOG had exposure,” Boolani posited.

For Fastly, which Boolani has a Neutral rating on, the company has “steadily” rebuilt credibility in its turnaround, but there is still more to go. 

“With continued improvements in sales efficiency, our views on business fundamentals/execution also improve and see opportunity for the company to yield balanced estimate surprises, which should support [low-single-digit] EV/S’26 valuation for an asset sustaining [mid-single-digit] topline CAGR that is concurrently delivering profitability improvements,” Boolani explained. “While the turnaround story is becoming increasingly intriguing, recent execution missteps, large customer headwinds, new c-suite (CEO, CFO, CRO), customer concentration magnitudes, lack of scale/differentiation/breadth/brand visibility in security, and the continued cash burn (albeit improving) tempers our enthusiasm and keeps us Neutral rated.”

data.title: Matthews International reaches agreement with activist Barington
data.description:
Matthews International (MATW) reached an agreement with activist Barington Capital and the activist agreed to withdraw its previously submitted director nominations.

Under the agreement with Matthews, Barington will abide by standstill provisions and certain other restrictions, according to a  statement on Thursday.

"We appreciate the dialogue that we have had with the Matthews Board and are pleased to have reached an agreement," James A. Mitarotonda, Chairman, President and CEO of Barington said in the statement. "We believe that Matthews' continuing strategic review, value creation plan and corporate governance changes are critical in achieving greater near- and long-term value for all shareholders."

Shares of Matthews fell 1.8%.

data.title: JPMorgan's new team helps firms raise private capital as IPO alternative - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) is forming a new advisory team focused on helping companies raise private capital, offering an alternative to public listings as the investment bank seeks to further capitalize on the private markets boom, according to a Friday media report.

The new, hybrid Private Capital Advisory and Solutions group advises on mergers-and-acquisitions-related activities while collaborating alongside the capital markets unit, the Wall Street Journal reported.

Connecting investors with firms seeking to raise private capital, bankers will reportedly advise clients on early-stage equity, preferred stock and convertible debt, and will also work on secondary funds and related liquidity solutions for private equity firms.

JPMorgan (JPM) is already a sizable player in private markets. Its private equity unit recently raised $1.44B for its 12th flagship fund, well above target.

"The private markets have just dwarfed the public markets lately," said Keith Canton, co-head of equity capital markets in the Americas, who will lead the new unit, the WSJ reported. "Historically, you only needed to think about an IPO or a sale as an exit. But there's much more you can do in the middle."

JPMorgan (JPM) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's request for comment.

data.title: Riot declares actions to cement data center developer position, pact with Advanced Micro Devices
data.description:
Riot Platforms (RIOT) was trading higher during morning trading on Friday after the bitcoin miner announced a series of transformative transactions for its data center business.

Shares were +7.60% to $17.83 after the opening bell.

The company acquired 200 acres of land at Rockdale for $96.0M for addition to its portfolio of data center development opportunities. The acquisition was funded entirely by the sale of ~1,080 bitcoins from its balance sheet.

As of December 2025-end, the company held 18,005 bitcoins.

Riot said it intends to convert the full 700 MW of gross power capacity at its Rockdale site for data center tenants.

Additionally, Riot signed a data center lease and services agreement with Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) at Rockdale.

The agreement includes an initial deployment of 25 MW of critical IT load capacity to be delivered in phases at the Rockdale site beginning in January 2026 and completing in May 2026. There is a potential for additional expansion of up to 200 MW of critical IT load capacity.

The data center lease carries an initial term of 10 years and is expected to generate ~$311M in contract revenue. The agreement includes three five-year extension options, which could bring the total expected contract revenue to ~$1B.

"I am pleased to share the substantial progress we have made at Rockdale, which expands our fully approved data center power portfolio to 1.7 gigawatts and, through the signing of a data center lease with AMD, firmly establishes Rockdale as a leading data center development opportunity," said Riot CEO Jason Les.

"We continue to work towards converting all available power capacity at our Rockdale and Corsicana sites for data center use," said the CEO.
